question: What makes LED-WS2812B-5050R100 unique?
answer:
The WS2812B LED SMD 5050 has a built-in driver IC, is chainable, and uses a single-wire NZR data protocol at 800 kbps.

[LED-WS2812B-5050R100.md]
## Description
WS2812B integrated RGB LED with built-in driver IC in a 5050 SMD package. Single-wire NZR data protocol at 800 kbps. Chainable — each LED in a strip controlled independently with 24-bit color (8 bits per channel). Supplied on a reel of 100 units.

[LED-WS2812B-5050R100.md]
## Q&A
**Q: How are WS2812B LEDs controlled?**
A: Via a single-wire NZR serial protocol at 800 kbps. Each LED shifts its 24-bit color data and passes the remainder to the next LED in the chain.

[LED-WS2812B-5050R100.md]
## Q&A
**Q: Can WS2812B be driven from a 3.3V MCU?**
A: The data input requires a logic-high voltage near 5V for reliable operation. Use a level shifter (e.g. 74AHCT125) between the 3.3V MCU GPIO and the 5V LED data line.

[LED-WS2812B-5050R100.md]
## Q&A
**Q: Do we have addressable RGB LEDs in stock?**
A: Yes — LED-WS2812B-5050R100 is a reel of 100 WS2812B addressable RGB LEDs.
